summary:
The patient has a net filtration pressure of 14 mmHg based on their capillary hydrostatic pressure being 42 mm Hg, interstitial fluid colloid oncotic pressure at 12 mm Hg, and Bowman’s capsule colloids having an effective negative value due to assumed absence of proteins. This result is calculated using appropriate physiological formulas reflecting normal kidney function parameters without considering solute effects or other complicating factors like plasma albumin levels which are not specified here. To summarize further:

The net filtration pressure was determined by subtracting the total胶体渗透压(pColli) between blood vessels and surrounding tissues from the arterial end-capillary hydrostatic pressures; specifically, it equaled 30 minus(-)16 resulting in positive force facilitating ultrafiltration across glomerular membranes into Bowman's space amounting precisely to 14 millimeters mercury gauge units indicating efficient renal clearance mechanisms operating normally within these measured ranges absent any pathological interference noted thus far during evaluation process conducted herein today. ``` 
In conclusion,

formatted_summary:
agent1 (Round 2 summarized): The patient has a net filtration pressure of 14 mmHg based on their capillary hydrostatic pressure being 42 mm Hg, interstitial fluid colloid oncotic pressure at 12 mm Hg, and Bowman’s capsule colloids having an effective negative value due to assumed absence of proteins. This result is calculated using appropriate physiological formulas reflecting normal kidney function parameters without considering solute effects or other complicating factors like plasma albumin levels which are not specified here. To summarize further:

The net filtration pressure was determined by subtracting the total胶体渗透压(pColli) between blood vessels and surrounding tissues from the arterial end-capillary hydrostatic pressures; specifically, it equaled 30 minus(-)16 resulting in positive force facilitating ultrafiltration across glomerular membranes into Bowman's space amounting precisely to 14 millimeters mercury gauge units indicating efficient renal clearance mechanisms operating normally within these measured ranges absent any pathological interference noted thus far during evaluation process conducted herein today. ``` 
In conclusion,
